body:
## Summary

Adds `@dsikeres1` registry to the registry directory.

**Registry:** [react-date-range-picker](https://dsikeres1.github.io/react-date-range-picker/)

A headless, composable date & date range picker for React with:
- 5 picker types (Date, DateRange, DateTime, DateRangeTime, Time)
- Compound Component API
- 15 locales, dark mode, keyboard navigation, accessibility
- Zero dependencies (headless core)
- Tailwind CSS v4 first-class support

**Registry URL:** `https://dsikeres1.github.io/react-date-range-picker/r/{name}.json`

**Components:**
- `date-picker` — Single date picker
- `date-range-picker` — Dual calendar range picker
- `date-time-picker` — Date + time picker
- `date-range-time-picker` — Range + time picker
- `time-picker` — Standalone time picker

**npm:** [react-date-range-picker-headless](https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-date-range-picker-headless) (v1.0.2)

**Source:** [github.com/dsikeres1/react-date-range-picker](https://github.com/dsikeres1/react-date-range-picker) (MIT)

title: fix(tests): fix e2e sleep
body:
The first e2e test was flaky on CI because `start-server-and-test` only checks that the root URL (http://localhost:4000) responds before running tests, not the /r registry endpoint. The existing 2-second hardcoded sleep in the first test was unreliable on slower CI runners.

Move the readiness check into the vitest globalSetup so all tests wait for the registry /r endpoint to actually be reachable before any test starts.
author: shadcn

commits.message:
fix(tests): wait for registry readiness in global setup instead of per-test sleep

The first e2e test was flaky on CI because `start-server-and-test` only
checks that the root URL (http://localhost:4000) responds before running
tests, not the /r registry endpoint. The existing 2-second hardcoded sleep
in the first test was unreliable on slower CI runners.

Move the readiness check into the vitest globalSetup so all tests wait for
the registry /r endpoint to actually be reachable before any test starts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(tests): fix race condition in global setup - poll correct URL and CLI binary

Two issues caused the previous fix to fail:

1. Was polling `http://localhost:4000/r` which is a directory → always 404.
   Now polls `{REGISTRY_URL}/index.json`, a real static file that returns 200.

2. The v4 dev script (`pnpm --filter=shadcn build && pnpm icons:dev & next dev`)
   runs the shadcn CLI build in the background while next dev starts immediately.
   On fast CI runs start-server-and-test can detect the server as ready before
   the CLI binary (packages/shadcn/dist/index.js) has been built, causing the
   first test to fail when it tries to invoke the CLI.
   Now explicitly waits for the binary to exist before any test runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(tests): warm up /init route in global setup to prevent first-test timeout

The CLI's first request during `shadcn init` hits the dynamic Next.js /init
route. On a cold dev server this route takes ~1.8s to compile. Combined with
the rest of what init does (pnpm install, file writes), this pushes the first
test over the 30s CLI timeout on CI. Subsequent tests pass because the route
is already warm.

Polling /init in global setup ensures the route is compiled before any test
runs, making the first test's CLI invocation as fast as all subsequent ones.

Also replaced the /r/index.json poll (a static file that responds immediately
and doesn't reflect real route readiness) with the actual /init route poll,
which also naturally verifies the registry server is up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(tests): warm up 404 route and increase default CLI timeout

Two more issues found in CI logs:

1. The CLI requests font files that don't exist (e.g. /r/styles/new-york-v4/
   font-geist.json), causing Next.js to compile /_not-found/page on the first
   404 response. That compilation takes ~4-5s on a cold dev server and is
   another hidden cost on the first test. Now triggering a 404 in global setup
   so the not-found page is compiled before any test runs.

2. The default CLI timeout of 30s is too tight for CI. Even with the /init and
   404 routes pre-warmed, pnpm install inside the fixture takes ~25s, leaving
   only ~5s of headroom. Increasing the default from 30s to 60s gives a
   comfortable buffer without masking real hangs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

// After label (end position)

```

title: Add fontsource and support override for registry:font install
body:
When shadcn add installs a registry:font in a non-Next.js project (Vite, React Router, Astro, etc.), it always uses `@fontsource-variable/{fontName}` 
This causes two problems:                                                                                                    
1. Not all fonts have a @fontsource-variable/ package. For example, **Lato** isn’t available in `fontsource/variable` - it’s available in `fontsource`                              
2. `@fontsource-variable/ packages` register fonts with a "Variable" suffix (e.g., "Inter Variable" instead of "Inter"), which may not match what users expect 
3. 
Goal: Install both `@fontsource/{fontName}` (base) and `@fontsource-variable/{fontName}` (variable), using CSS `@supports` so that variable fonts are loaded only in clients that support them.

title: fix: monorepo templates ignore the user's package manager
body:
Fixes #7695

All monorepo templates hardcode `packageManager: "pnpm"` which overrides
`getPackageManager()` entirely. Running `bunx --bun shadcn init --monorepo`
still shells out to `pnpm install`, Corepack tries to kick in, and Bun
crashes on `process.mainModule = void 0` (readonly in Bun's runtime).

Repro:

```
bunx --bun shadcn@latest init --template start --monorepo

TypeError: Attempted to assign to readonly property.
  at runVersion (corepack.cjs:22426:3)
```

This removes the hardcoded overrides and lets detection work. Since the
templates on disk are pnpm-structured, the scaffold step now converts
them after cloning when a different PM is detected:

- strips `"packageManager"` from package.json (stops Corepack)
- moves workspace globs from `pnpm-workspace.yaml` into package.json `"workspaces"`
- removes `pnpm-lock.yaml`
- rewrites `workspace:*` to `"*"` for npm (bun/yarn handle it natively)
- passes `--no-frozen-lockfile` only for pnpm, nothing extra otherwise

When pnpm is detected nothing runs — early return, zero change in behavior.
Non-monorepo templates are also unaffected (conversion is gated on
`pnpm-workspace.yaml` existing).

All existing shadcn tests pass with zero changes needed to any other test
file (60/60 files, 1197/1197 tests). Updated scaffold.test.ts to cover
the new per-PM install arg behavior. Also tested end-to-end locally with
bun, pnpm, and npm monorepo init.
author: devinscodebase

commits.message:
fix: respect detected package manager for monorepo templates

All monorepo templates hardcoded `packageManager: "pnpm"` which
meant running `bunx --bun shadcn@latest init --monorepo` would
still shell out to `pnpm install`, triggering Corepack and crashing
under Bun because `process.mainModule` is readonly there.

This removes the hardcoded pnpm override from every monorepo template
config so `getPackageManager()` can actually detect what the user is
running. The scaffold step now adapts the cloned template on the fly:

- strips the `packageManager` field from package.json (avoids Corepack)
- converts pnpm-workspace.yaml to a `"workspaces"` array in package.json
- removes pnpm-lock.yaml
- rewrites `workspace:*` refs to `"*"` when the detected PM is npm
  (npm doesn't support the workspace: protocol)
- picks the right install flags per PM (`--no-frozen-lockfile` for pnpm,
  nothing extra for bun/npm/yarn)

pnpm behavior is completely unchanged — `adaptWorkspaceConfig` early-
returns when the detected PM is pnpm.
style: fix prettier formatting in create-template.ts

title: feat(registry): add @jalco registry
body:
## Add @jalco to the open source registry index

**Registry:** [@jalco](https://ui.justinlevine.me)
**URL pattern:** `https://ui.justinlevine.me/r/{name}.json`

### About

Jalco UI is a curated collection of GitHub-integrated, documentation, and developer-facing components. Self-contained, zero-dependency, and production-ready.

### Components

- **ActivityGraph** — GitHub-style contribution heatmap with auto-fit sizing and custom color scales
- **GitHubStarsButton** — Live star count button with multiple variants and icon styles
- **GitHubButtonGroup** — Grouped GitHub repo metrics (stars, forks, watchers, issues)
- **CodeBlock** — Syntax-highlighted code block with language icon, copy button, and collapsible overflow
- **CodeBlockCommand** — Multi-package-manager install command with tab switching
- **CodeLine** — Single-line code snippet with inline copy button
- **AiCopyButton** — Copy-to-clipboard button with AI-provider icon cycling
- **RequestViewer** — HTTP request display with method badge, URL, headers, and body
- **ApiRefTable** — Expandable prop reference table for component docs
- **TipJar** — Crypto tip jar with QR code, address copy, and wallet deep links

### Checklist

- [x] Registry is open source and publicly accessible
- [x] Valid JSON conforming to the registry schema
- [x] Flat registry structure (`/r/registry.json` and `/r/{name}.json`)
- [x] `files` array in registry.json does NOT include `content` property
- [x] Entry added to `apps/v4/registry/directory.json`

title: feat: add @gc-solid registry for SolidJS components
body:
# Add @gc-solid registry for SolidJS components

## Description

This PR adds the `@gc-solid` registry to the official shadcn registry index, providing a complete port of shadcn-ui components to SolidJS.

## Registry Details

- **Name**: `@gc-solid`
- **URL**: https://binnodon.github.io/gc-solid-ui/r/{name}.json
- **Homepage**: https://binnodon.github.io/gc-solid-ui
- **Framework**: SolidJS
- **Components**: 57 UI components
- **Primitives**: Built on Kobalte (SolidJS accessibility primitives)
- **Theme**: Vega (shadcn neutral theme)
- **TypeScript**: Full type safety

## Available Components

Accordion, Alert, Alert Dialog, Aspect Ratio, Avatar, Badge, Breadcrumb, Button, Button Group, Calendar, Card, Carousel, Chart, Checkbox, Collapsible, Combobox, Command, Context Menu, Date Picker, Dialog, Direction, Drawer, Dropdown Menu, Empty, Field, Form, Hover Card, Input, Input Group, Input OTP, Item, Kbd, Label, Menubar, Native Select, Navigation Menu, Pagination, Popover, Progress, Radio Group, Resizable, Scroll Area, Select, Separator, Sheet, Sidebar, Skeleton, Slider, Sonner, Spinner, Switch, Table, Tabs, Textarea, Toggle, Toggle Group, Tooltip

## Registry Validation

✅ **Open source and publicly accessible**
- Repository: https://github.com/binnodon/gc-solid-ui
- License: MIT

✅ **Valid JSON conforming to registry schema**
- Schema: `https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry.json`
- Validated: All component JSONs include proper `$schema` references

✅ **Flat structure**
- Root: `/registry.json` at https://binnodon.github.io/gc-solid-ui/r/registry.json
- Components: `/r/{name}.json` pattern

✅ **No embedded content in registry.json**
- Registry index contains only metadata
- Content is in individual component JSON files

## Testing

Direct URL installation verified:
```bash
npx shadcn@latest add https://binnodon.github.io/gc-solid-ui/r/button.json
```

Components correctly install with:
- SolidJS imports (not React)
- Kobalte primitives
- Proper TypeScript types
- Correct import path transformations

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](https://binnodon.github.io/gc-solid-ui/#-installation)
- [Component Documentation](https://binnodon.github.io/gc-solid-ui/)
- [Source Repository](https://github.com/binnodon/gc-solid-ui)

## Attribution

Forked from the excellent work at [solid-ui](https://github.com/stefan-karger/solid-ui) with components ported from shadcn-ui.

---

**Checklist:**
- [x] Registry is open source and publicly accessible
- [x] Registry conforms to schema specification
- [x] Flat structure with registry.json at root
- [x] Files array does not include content property
- [x] All components tested and working
- [x] Documentation is complete
- [x] Added to directory.json alphabetically
author: binnodon

title: feat(registry): add @lmscn
body:
Add lmscn registry

lmscn is an open source registry of LMS (Learning Management System) components built with shadcn/ui. It provides ready-to-use interactive learning components for building educational applications.
Components included:

Quiz — Multi-type quiz (single-choice, multi-select, true/false) with per-question scoring, progress bar and pass/fail results
Flashcards — CSS 3D flip-card deck with optional self-rating (Again / Hard / Good / Easy)
Match — Click-to-match term/definition pairs with mistake tracking
Fill in the Blank — Inline blank-filling exercise with multiple blanks and keyboard navigation
Word Scramble — Letter-tile word unscrambling game with optional image/text clues
Order / Sequence — Drag-and-drop item-sequencing exercise
Reading Passage — Three-phase reading comprehension with passage view, inline questions and results
Progress Tracker — Gamified zigzag lesson map with XP bar, level badge and daily streak
Spaced Repetition — SM-2 algorithm review session with interval and ease factor computation
Image Hotspot — Click labelled markers on a diagram or photo and type answers in a popover

Registry URL: https://lmscn.vercel.app/r/registry.json

title: fix(base-sidebar): fix tooltip rendering with render prop
body:
## Description

Fixes an issue where `SidebarMenuButton` ignored the `render` prop when a `tooltip` was provided.

Previously, when `tooltip` was set, the component rendered `TooltipTrigger` but failed to forward the `render` prop. As a result, custom elements (e.g. `<a>` tags or `Link` components) were not preserved and could render incorrectly.

This change ensures that `render` is consistently forwarded through `TooltipTrigger`, preserving custom render behavior even when tooltips are enabled.


## Changes

- Forward `render` to `TooltipTrigger` in `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/sidebar.tsx`.
- Updated base examples for consistency.
- Regenerated all registry JSON files via `pnpm registry:build`.


## Verification

- Confirmed that `render` is correctly applied with and without `tooltip`.
- Verified consistent behavior across all base styles: `vega`, `nova`, `maia`, `lyra`, and `mira`.
- Ran `pnpm registry:build` to ensure generated files match source.
author: nkurunziza-saddy

commits.message: fix(base-sidebar): fix tooltip rendering with render prop

title: fix the missing items in select page (base ui)
body: fixes: #9753 According to the Base UI Select documentation, the `items` prop must be explicitly provided to the `Select` component. If it is not passed, the component falls back to using the label instead of the actual value, which leads to incorrect rendering behavior. [Docs](https://base-ui.com/react/components/select#api-reference) - This PR adds the missing `items` prop to the Select component to remove the unintended fallback behavior
author: Shitanshukumar607

title: fix(notion-prompt-form): replace w-48 with min-w-48 on model dropdown
body:
## Fix: Model Selector Dropdown Width

### What  
Replaces `w-48` with `min-w-48` on the model selector `DropdownMenuContent` in `NotionPromptForm`.

### Why  
The fixed `w-48` (192px) clamps the dropdown to a hard width. When a row contains a `Badge` alongside the label, for example `"Agent Mode"` + `"Beta"`, the row content becomes wider than 192px.

This causes the focus and hover highlight to get visually cut off at the right edge.

Using `min-w-48` preserves the minimum width constraint while allowing the dropdown to expand to fit its content.

### Before / After

| Before (`w-48`) | After (`min-w-48`) |
|-----------------|--------------------|
| Hover highlight clipped on `"Agent Mode"` row | Hover highlight renders fully across the row |

### Visual Reference

![Dropdown width issue](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3cae66c5-ebde-4bda-bdfc-98fae13c554f)

### Changes

**File:**  
`apps/v4/app/(app)/(root)/components/notion-prompt-form.tsx`

```diff
- <DropdownMenuContent className="w-48">
+ <DropdownMenuContent className="min-w-48">
author: myusubov

commits.message: fix(notion-prompt-form): replace w-48 with min-w-48 on model dropdown to prevent hover clipping

title: feat(shadcn): add HTTP 410 (Gone) support to registry fetcher
body:
Add explicit support for HTTP status code **410 (Gone)** in the [registry fetcher](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/packages/shadcn/src/registry/fetcher.ts) to differentiate permanently removed resources from not found.

**What I did in this PR**
- Add GONE error code to RegistryErrorCode enum
- Add RegistryGoneError class with appropriate message and suggestion
- Handle 410 responses in fetchRegistry function
- Add tests for 410 error handling

**Real Use Case**
This enables registries like pastecn to properly indicate that a resource has been permanently removed (e.g., expired snippets), providing users with semantically correct error messages.

I'm using 404 which works fine. But semantically speaking, 410 Gone is more accurate in my opinion.
author: rbadillap

commits.message: feat(shadcn): add HTTP 410 (Gone) support to registry fetcher

title: fix: update theme toggle key detection to exclude Alt key
body:
Fixes: #9198
fix: avoid overriding browser ALT + D shortcut

This PR fixes an accessibility and UX issue where ALT + D was overriding the browser’s default shortcut behavior.

On Chrome (Windows and Linux), ALT + D is reserved for focusing the address bar. The site was intercepting this shortcut to toggle the theme between light and dark mode. This change prevents the site from overriding the browser default behavior.

Affected pages:

Documentation pages (e.g. /docs/installation/react-router)

title: feat(registry): add @boldkit neubrutalism components
body:
## Summary

Adds **BoldKit** to the registry index - a neubrutalism component library built on shadcn/ui with React, Vue, and Nuxt support.

## Registry Details

- **Name:** @boldkit
- **Homepage:** https://boldkit.dev
- **Registry URL:** https://boldkit.dev/r/{name}.json

## What is BoldKit?

BoldKit is a neubrutalism-styled component library featuring:

- **43 Components** - All shadcn/ui components restyled with neubrutalism aesthetics (thick 3px borders, hard shadows, bold colors, zero border-radius)
- **42 SVG Shapes** - Unique decorative shapes (Burst, Heart, Lightning, Star, Shield, Cloud, Sun, Moon, Planet, Rainbow, etc.) for badges, stickers, and visual accents
- **Special Components** - Marquee (scrolling ticker), Sticker (rotated labels), Layered Card (stacked paper effect)
- **Framework Support** - React, Vue 3, and Nuxt
- **Full Accessibility** - Built on Radix UI (React) / Reka UI (Vue) primitives
- **Dark Mode Support** - Complete theme with light/dark modes
- **TypeScript** - Fully typed components

## Installation Examples

```bash
# Install a component
npx shadcn@latest add https://boldkit.dev/r/button.json

# Install shapes (42 SVG shapes)
npx shadcn@latest add https://boldkit.dev/r/shapes.json

# Install theme
npx shadcn@latest add https://boldkit.dev/r/theme.json
```

## Available Components

accordion, alert, alert-dialog, aspect-ratio, avatar, badge, breadcrumb, button, calendar, card, chart, checkbox, collapsible, command, dialog, drawer, dropdown-menu, hover-card, input, input-otp, label, layered-card, marquee, pagination, popover, progress, radio-group, scroll-area, select, separator, shapes, sheet, skeleton, slider, sonner, sticker, switch, table, tabs, textarea, toggle, toggle-group, tooltip

## Links

- Documentation: https://boldkit.dev/docs
- Components: https://boldkit.dev/components
- Shapes: https://boldkit.dev/shapes (42 unique SVG shapes)
- Charts: https://boldkit.dev/charts
- GitHub: https://github.com/ANIBIT14/boldkit
- Release: https://github.com/ANIBIT14/boldkit/releases/tag/v2.3.0

## Checklist

- [x] Registry is publicly accessible
- [x] Registry follows the shadcn schema
- [x] All 43 components work with shadcn CLI
- [x] Logo uses var(--foreground) and var(--background) for theming
- [x] MIT Licensed
- [x] Ran `pnpm registry:build` and committed registries.json

title: fix(docs): improve TanStack Form example performance
body:
## Summary
- Change validation strategy from `onChange` to `onBlur` in the ExampleForm component
- Prevents full Zod schema validation on every keystroke

## Problem
The ExampleForm in the internal sink uses `validators: { onChange: exampleFormSchema }`, which validates the entire Zod schema on every keystroke. The schema contains expensive operations (regex checks, date calculations, refine() calls), causing noticeable input lag when typing in text fields.

**Note:** The GitHub issue mentions the "Bug Report Form", but that form (`form-tanstack-demo.tsx`) already uses `onSubmit` validation, which is correct. The actual performance issue is in `example-form.tsx`.

## Solution
Changed validation to `onBlur` strategy:
- Validates when a field loses focus (user tabs/clicks away)
- User can type freely without lag
- Matches the pattern used in other TanStack Form examples (e.g., `form-tanstack-array.tsx`)

## File Changed
- `apps/v4/app/(internal)/sink/(pages)/tanstack-form/example-form.tsx` (line 80)

## Test plan
- [x] Verified no TypeScript errors
- [ ] Type rapidly in the "Additional Comments" textarea - should have no lag

Fixes #9222
author: PhilemonChiro

commits.message:
fix(docs): improve TanStack Form example performance

Change validation strategy from onChange to onBlur to prevent
validation on every keystroke, which causes input lag in textareas.

title: fix(resizable): upgrade to react-resizable-panels v4
body:
Fixes #9118, #9124, #9136, #9200

`react-resizable-panels` v4 renamed exports and changed APIs, so CLI users were getting build errors like:

```
error TS2339: Property 'PanelGroup' does not exist...
error TS2339: Property 'PanelResizeHandle' does not exist...
```

Picking up from #9124 (bvaughn's PR). He couldn't continue due to lint/test issues on main, so I applied his changes with the CSS selector fix that stevezhu pointed out in the review.

### What changed

- `PanelGroup` → `Group`, `PanelResizeHandle` → `Separator`
- `direction` → `orientation`
- `defaultSize={50}` → `defaultSize="50%"`
- CSS: `data-[panel-group-direction=vertical]` → `aria-[orientation=horizontal]` for Separator (flipped)
- `onLayout` → `onLayoutChange` with `Layout` type

### Testing

- `pnpm lint` (passed)
- `pnpm test` - 2 failures in `init.test.ts`, unrelated (pre-existing `buttonVariants` issue)
-  on web resize works, vertical/horizontal/nested all good
author: jaem0629

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the v4 app’s resizable components and registries to `react-resizable-panels` v4, aligning the API usage (Group/Separator/orientation/Layout) across UI components, examples, docs, and registry templates.

**Changes:**
- Replaced `PanelGroup`/`PanelResizeHandle` with `Group`/`Separator` (and their `*Props` types) and switched from `direction` to `orientation` across v4 resizable components and examples.
- Updated layout behavior to v4’s `Layout`/`onLayoutChange` model and changed `defaultSize`/`minSize` to percentage strings (e.g. `"50%"`).
- Adjusted CSS selectors from `data-[panel-group-direction=vertical]` to ARIA-based selectors and bumped `react-resizable-panels` in `apps/v4/package.json` to `^4`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 47 out of 47 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/resizable.tsx | Wraps `react-resizable-panels` v4 `Group`/`Panel`/`Separator` with updated props, ARIA-based orientation classes, and reordered exports. |
| apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/resizable-vertical.tsx | Example switched from `direction` to `orientation` and numeric `defaultSize` to percentage strings for vertical layout. |
| apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/resizable-handle.tsx | Horizontal example updated to v4 `orientation` and percentage `defaultSize` values with handle. |
| apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/resizable-demo.tsx | Nested horizontal/vertical demo updated to use `orientation` and percentage `defaultSize`. |
| apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/resizable-demo-with-handle.tsx | Same as above but with resize handles; now uses `orientation` and percentage sizes. |
| apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/resizable.tsx | Radix base resizable wrapper migrated to v4 `GroupProps`/`PanelProps`/`SeparatorProps` and ARIA-based selectors. |
| apps/v

## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 52 out of 53 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.
Files not reviewed (1) * **pnpm-lock.yaml**: Language not supported
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix(resizable): upgrade to react-resizable-panels v4

- Update component API: PanelGroup → Group, PanelResizeHandle → Separator
- Update prop: direction → orientation
- Update size values: number → string with units (e.g., "50%")
- Update CSS selectors: data-[panel-group-direction] → aria-[orientation]
- Update controlled component: onLayout → onLayoutChange with Layout type

Closes #9118, #9136, #9200
fix: update props, migrate components and add changelog
Merge branch 'fix/resizable-v4-upgrade' of github.com:jaem0629/ui into fix/resizable-v4-upgrade

title: fix: allow vertical scroll pass-through on code blocks
body:
Fixes #9446

Code snippets in docs were trapping scroll events, preventing page scroll when the mouse was over a code block.

The `pre` elements had `overscroll-none` applied, which prevents scroll event propagation. This caused vertical scroll to be trapped inside code blocks even when they had no vertical overflow.

So I changed `overscroll-none` to `overflow-y-auto overscroll-x-contain overscroll-y-auto`:

- `overscroll-x-contain`: Prevents horizontal scroll from bubbling
- `overscroll-y-auto`: Allows vertical scroll to pass through to parent when code block can't scroll, or chains to parent at scroll boundaries
- `overflow-y-auto` to properly handle code blocks with height constraints

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f5ff2a0c-993c-4ca8-af28-29f98e325eb9
author: jaem0629

commits.message: fix: allow vertical scroll pass-through on code blocks

title: fix(components): use DialogOverlay in CommandMenu to fix button interactions
body:
Fixes #9403

## Description

This PR fixes a bug where buttons on documentation pages (e.g., Sonner demo, Input Group demos) were not working. The issue affected all browsers and was caused by improper overlay lifecycle management in the `CommandMenu` component.

## Root Cause

The `CommandMenu` component used `DialogPrimitive.Overlay` directly instead of the standard `DialogOverlay` component. The standard `DialogOverlay` includes critical animation state classes (`data-[state=open]:animate-in`, `data-[state=closed]:animate-out`, etc.) that Radix uses to manage the overlay lifecycle.

Without these state transitions, `react-remove-scroll` could get into a corrupted global state, causing the `inert` attribute to remain on page content and blocking all interactions.

## Changes

- Added `DialogOverlay` to imports from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/dialog`
- Replaced `DialogPrimitive.Overlay` with `DialogOverlay` in the custom `DialogContent` function

## Testing

1. Clear `localStorage` completely (including `shadcn-create-welcome-dialog`)
2. Refresh the page
3. Navigate to docs pages (e.g., `/docs/components/radix/sonner`)
4. Open and close the command menu (⌘K)
5. Click buttons on component demos — they should now work without needing to visit the Create page first
author: DallasCarraher

commits.message:
fix(components): use DialogOverlay in CommandMenu to fix button interactions

Replace DialogPrimitive.Overlay with the standard DialogOverlay component
to properly manage the overlay lifecycle with correct animation state
classes. This fixes issue #9403 where buttons on documentation pages
were not working due to react-remove-scroll getting into a corrupted
global state.

This PR updates the `@motion-primitives` registry URL to include the required `{name}` placeholder.

The previous URL was set to a static path (`https://motion-primitives.com/c/registry.json`), which caused the CLI validation to fail because it could not resolve individual component paths dynamically.

title: docs(theming): add missing `destructive-foreground` CSS variable
body:
## Summary

Fixes #9337

- Added the missing `--destructive-foreground` CSS variable to the theming documentation

## Problem

The `destructive-foreground` CSS variable is used in components (e.g., for text on destructive buttons) but was missing from the theming guide documentation. This made it harder for users to customize the destructive state colors completely.

## Solution

Added `--destructive-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0);` (white/light color for readability on red backgrounds) to:
- Main "List of variables" section (both `:root` and `.dark`)
- All 5 base color schemes: Neutral, Stone, Zinc, Gray, Slate (both light and dark modes)

## Changes

```diff
  --destructive: oklch(0.577 0.245 27.325);
+ --destructive-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0);
  --border: oklch(0.922 0 0);
```

## Test Plan

- [x] Verified all color schemes now include `--destructive-foreground`
- [x] Value follows the same pattern as other foreground variables
author: sunnypatell

title: Fix: Preserve 'use client' directive in universal registry items
body:
Fixes #8432

## Problem

Universal registry items (`registry:file` and `registry:item`) are designed to be framework-agnostic components that can be installed without running `shadcn init` or having a `components.json` file. These items should preserve their original content as-is.

However, the `transformRsc` transformer was incorrectly removing `'use client'` directives from universal item files when `config.rsc` was `false` or `undefined`. This broke client-side functionality in universal items that require React hooks or browser APIs.

### Why this happens:
1. Universal items can be installed without project setup
2. When no config exists or `rsc` is `false` by default, the RSC transformer runs
3. The transformer removes `'use client'` from ALL files, including universal items
4. This breaks the original functionality of universal items

## Solution

Skip all transformers for universal registry items (`registry:file` and `registry:item` types) to preserve their original content, similar to how `.env` files are handled.

Universal items are meant to be copied exactly as-is from the registry, so they should bypass:
- RSC transformer
- Import transformer
- CSS variables transformer
- Tailwind prefix transformer
- Icon transformer
- Next.js middleware transformer

Regular shadcn components (`registry:ui`, `registry:lib`, etc.) continue to have transformers applied as expected.

## Changes

### Modified Files

**`packages/shadcn/src/utils/updaters/update-files.ts`**
- Added check to identify universal item files before applying transformers
- Skip all transformers for `registry:file` and `registry:item` types
- Preserve original content including `'use client'` directives

**`packages/shadcn/test/utils/updaters/update-files.test.ts`**
- Added test: verify `'use client'` is preserved in `registry:file` items
- Added test: verify `'use client'` is preserved in `registry:item` items
- Added test: verify `'use client'` is still removed from regular components when `rsc: false`

## Testing

The fix includes comprehensive tests to ensure:
1. ✅ Universal items (`registry:file`) preserve `'use client'` directives
2. ✅ Universal items (`registry:item`) preserve `'use client'` directives
3. ✅ Regular components still have transformers applied correctly
4. ✅ Existing functionality for non-universal items remains unchanged

## Impact

- **Universal items**: Now work correctly without project initialization
- **Regular components**: No change in behavior
- **Backward compatibility**: Fully maintained

This ensures universal items work as intended - as standalone, framework-agnostic components that can be copied and used immediately without any transformations.
author: VeiaG

commits.message:
fix: preserve 'use client' directive in universal registry items

Universal items (registry:file and registry:item) are framework-agnostic
components that can be installed without shadcn project initialization.
However, the RSC transformer was incorrectly removing 'use client'
directives from these files when config.rsc was false/undefined, breaking
client-side functionality.

This fix ensures transformers are skipped for universal items, preserving
their original content including 'use client' directives, while regular
shadcn components continue to have transformers applied as expected.

Changes:
- Skip all transformers for registry:file and registry:item types
- Add tests to verify 'use client' preservation in universal items
- Ensure regular components still have transformers applied

Fixes issue where universal items would lose 'use client' directives when
copied without a full shadcn project setup.

title: fix(cli): validate project name using npm package name rules
body: Fixes #9160 ## Problem When creating a new project using `npx shadcn@latest create`, the CLI accepts uppercase letters in the project name but then fails with a generic error message: > Something went wrong creating a new Next.js project. Please try again. This happens because npm package names cannot contain uppercase letters, but the CLI wasn't validating this before attempting to create the project. ## Solution Added proper npm package name validation using the `validate-npm-package-name` package. This validates the project name against npm's naming rules and provides clear, actionable error messages when validation fails. ## Reference: Next CLI validation: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/packages/create-next-app/helpers/validate-pkg.ts NextJS requires this naming convention, although `vite-react` and `tantack-start` might not need this validation. So, we have two options: 1. Validate only if NextJS is selected 2. Validate for all frameworks Option 1 does not seem like a proper solution, so I chose `option 2`, awaiting opinions or reviews from @shadcn or team members. ### Changes - Added `validate-npm-package-name` dependency and its types - Created `packages/shadcn/src/utils/validate-pkg.ts` utility for name validation - Updated `packages/shadcn/src/commands/create.ts` to use the new validation in the project name prompt ### Before ### After
author: yeasin2002

reviews.body: I made some minor changes and apply the validation for all frameworks (for consistency). Thank you.

title: fix(base-ui): resolve pagination example and button client boundary issues
body:
### Button
In a production-ready site using SSG, the Base UI button (which is a client component) was not being treated as a client component when rendered through several layers of server components, which caused an "Element type is invalid error". Marking the wrapper as `"use client"` resolves the issue.

Note:
I confirmed this using real SSG pages in a fresh Next.js project with the same pagination components used in isolation.

### Pagination Example
The pagination example is also marked as a Client Component. This ensures it participates in the correct client boundary and prevents the same error.

Similar: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/issues/9025, https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/pull/9027
author: rainchuk

commits.message: fix(pagination-example): mark example as client component
fix(button): mark wrapper as client since Base UI button is a client component

Here issue: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/issues/9025

When selecting the Base UI system, the Pagination component crashes. Locally I got this error:

> Base UI: A component that acts as a button was not rendered as a native <button>, which does not match the default. Ensure that the element passed to the render prop of the component is a real <button>, or set the nativeButton prop on the component to false.

So I added `nativeButton={false}` It might not be the best solution, but at least it should make the pagination render.

title: fix: preview iframe URL state sync
body:
Fixes an issue where previous themes would be stuck after switching items or base library.

There were a couple of issues:
- The preview iframe was fed a URL containing stale search params (`initialParams` were never updated)
- The useSyncExternalStore storage inside the iframe could opt out nuqs' state transitions out of concurrent mode (more of a performance thing than correctness).

The proposed refactor is to keep the iframe URL as the source of truth within it, and sync it to the host's URL from the outside:
1. On initial load, the current search params from the hosts are passed to the iframe src, to avoid a flash of unthemed content. This is not made reactive on search params to avoid a full-frame load (flashing & loss of local state).
2. When the host's search params change, they are forwarded to the iframe via `postMessage`. The iframe then applies those to its own internal URL, using nuqs (in shallow + replace mode) to benefit from the time-safety (URL throttling shared between host & iframe).

Note: some dead code was found and removed in the process:
- use-canva.tsx
- use-design-system.tsx (made redundant)

One thing I noticed while testing is that in production, pressing R to randomise emits two RSC requests (with identical search params aside from the _rsc marker), but only one in development.

Note: it also fixes an issue where pressing the Back button erased the ability to move forward. We can now use Back/Forward to time-travel navigate theme changes, allowing for A/B testing.
author: franky47

commits.message: fix: preview iframe URL state sync
fix: reset iframe on base change

This PR fixes `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` lint errors in combobox and scroll-area components.

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR resolves TypeScript ESLint warnings for unused variables in combobox and scroll-area UI components across both base and radix implementations. - Fixes unused `className` prop in `ScrollBar` component by properly merging it with default classes using the `cn` utility - Removes unused `children` prop destructuring from `ComboboxChipsInput` component ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/scroll-area.tsx | Applies `className` prop using `cn` utility in `ScrollBar` component | | apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/combobox.tsx | Removes unused `children` prop from `ComboboxChipsInput` function signature | | apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/scroll-area.tsx | Applies `className` prop using `cn` utility in `ScrollBar` component | | apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/combobox.tsx | Removes unused `children` prop from `ComboboxChipsInput` function signature | The changes are straightforward lint fixes with no functional side effects. Both fixes are correctly implemented: 1. The `ScrollBar` component now properly merges any custom `className` passed by consumers with the default classes 2. The `ComboboxChipsInput` component no longer destructures an unused `children` prop (the component passes props to `ComboboxPrimitive.Input` which handles children internally) All changes are consistent across both base and radix implementations, maintaining code uniformity across the component library. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

title: refactor: nuqs APIs
body:
Refactors a few things nuqs-related:

1. Replaces the cache with a loader - [diff](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/pull/9055/changes#diff-5c85213f6c9e19363f85b51fab71c28ce37d28adc9d92b9a048f222168890855L63-R63)

`createSearchParamsCache` is intended for deep access in nested RSCs without prop-drilling, but it's based on a hack (ab)using React.cache, that breaks with cacheComponents. It uses a loader internally, which is more lightweight and we can use in both RSCs and on the client, in the iframe sync logic.

2. Uses the [`inferParserType`](https://nuqs.dev/docs/utilities#parser-type-inference) type helper for extracting the state type from the search params descriptor object - [diff](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/pull/9055/changes#diff-9df24bbaa2a86b052835c7afa1e30e4126a01a5b472394f8eb24fe5f108fbbbdR83-R84)

3. Refactors the `useQueryStates` calls into a single definition - [diff](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/pull/9055/changes#diff-9df24bbaa2a86b052835c7afa1e30e4126a01a5b472394f8eb24fe5f108fbbbdR76-R81)

Rather than repeating the same options, they can be factored into the search-params descriptor file, with possible overrides (like the item that uses shallow: true, though there may be a better way to do this, see the [self-review](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/pull/9055/changes#r2616510255)).

4. Simplify the iframe sync logic

Since loaders can be used everywhere, they make quick work of de-duplicating the parsing logic for the iframe sync (in `getInitialValues`):
- When on the server, we load an empty string which returns all the default values
- When on the client, we just pass `location.search`

The only difference from the global defaults was the item set to `"cover-example"`, which I left as-is too (see [self-review](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/pull/9055/changes#r2616514243)).

## Other tasks

I also found a bug on `main` while testing this PR:
- Enter the app from a link, eg: https://ui.shadcn.com/create?font=jetbrains-mono&style=lyra&radius=none&menuAccent=bold&theme=amber
- Reset using the "Start Over" button
- Switch the component library from Radix to BaseUI
- The old theme is back

This behaves differently in dev & prod, I'll try and do a minimal repro of that one on the nuqs repo, could be a second PR.
author: franky47

title: fix: dependencies for Bun in `monorepo-next`
body:
https://github.com/vercel/turborepo/issues/11047 was opened in Turborepo, but this has to do with Bun's recent changes to dependency resolution. In short, Bun is more conservatively hoisting dependencies than other package managers, so we must be more explicit about declaring the dependencies for packages in the workspace.

This change will not negatively affect other package managers. Adding these dependencies is being more technically correct about which dependencies are getting used in which packages, so other package managers will continue working.

title: fix(shadcn): Restore two-finger navigation on macOS by adjusting overscroll behavior
body:
### Summary 
This PR resolves Two-finger navigation broken on macOS.
On macOS, the global CSS rule `@apply overscroll-none scroll-smooth;
` prevents native two-finger swipe gestures (back/forward navigation) in Safari and Chromium-based browsers.

To fix this, the overscroll behavior is now restricted to the Y-axis only:

```
- @apply overscroll-none scroll-smooth;
+ @apply overscroll-y-none;
```
### Why ?
Using overscroll-behavior: none; disables horizontal overscroll as well, which breaks browser navigation gestures.
Switching to `overscroll-y: none;` ensures:

✅ Vertical bounce is still prevented
✅ Horizontal navigation gestures work again on macOS

closes #8705
author: Harshul23

commits.message: fix(shadcn): Tow finger navigation on macOS
Add smooth scrolling to html element

title: Fix utils import transform when workspace alias does not start with `@`
body:
The code was assuming the workspace to always start with `@`, this is not always the case

Some snapshots from old tests also got updated with the correct values

Fix https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/issues/7348
author: remorses

reviews.body: This looks good. I'm going to test (some edge cases) and merge this. Thank you.

## Pull Request Overview This PR fixes an issue with utils import transformation where the code incorrectly assumed workspace aliases always start with `@`. The fix removes the hardcoded `@` prefix assumption and correctly handles workspace aliases that don't start with `@`. - Remove hardcoded `@` prefix assumption from workspace alias processing - Update test snapshots to reflect correct import paths - Add new test case for workspace aliases without `@` prefix ### Reviewed Changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | packages/shadcn/src/utils/transformers/transform-import.ts | Fixed workspace alias processing to not assume `@` prefix | | packages/shadcn/test/utils/transform-import.test.ts | Added test case for workspace folder without `@` prefix | | packages/shadcn/test/utils/__snapshots__/transform-import.test.ts.snap | Updated snapshots with corrected import paths | --- **Tip:** Customize your code reviews with copilot-instructions.md. Create the file or learn how to get started.

## Pull Request Overview Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

Bumps [jotai](https://github.com/pmndrs/jotai) from 2.13.1 to 2.15.0.
Release notes

Sourced from jotai's releases.

v2.15.0

This is another change in the internal functions of the core, to help Jotai ecosystem libraries.

What's Changed

  • feat(internals): External building blocks surface by @​dmaskasky in pmndrs/jotai#3149
  • breaking: drop atom.unstable_is by @​dai-shi in pmndrs/jotai#3150
  • add: read atom store hook and store hook tests by @​dmaskasky in pmndrs/jotai#3151

New Contributors

  • @​Antisunny made their first contribution in pmndrs/jotai#3155

Full Changelog: https://github.com/pmndrs/jotai/compare/v2.14.0...v2.15.0

v2.14.0

This version changes the internal functions of the core. Libraries that depend on them, such as jotai-devtools, need to be updated correspondingly.

What's Changed

  • refactor: buildingBlocks to storeState by @​dmaskasky in pmndrs/jotai#3105
  • allow atom with default update function to return reset and add tests by @​scamden in pmndrs/jotai#3137

New Contributors

  • @​peterpme made their first contribution in pmndrs/jotai#3132
  • @​01-binary made their first contribution in pmndrs/jotai#3138
  • @​scamden made their first contribution in pmndrs/jotai#3137

Full Changelog: https://github.com/pmndrs/jotai/compare/v2.13.1...v2.14.0

Commits
  • 4661064 2.15.0
  • 909f8cf chore: update dev dependencies (#3160)
  • 31f3802 add: read atom store hook and store hook tests (#3151)
  • 65e5f57 breaking: drop atom.unstable_is (#3150)
  • 74c1c2e feat(internals): External building blocks surface (#3149)
  • 5d53e54 verbose in ecosystem ci (#3156)
  • e77bb9a fix atomWithStorage missing style (#3155)
  • a87045a chore(eslint.config): replace deprecated 'tseslint.config' with 'defineConfig...
  • a2d0c19 2.14.0
  • 864814e chore: update dev dependencies (#3141)
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

reviews.body: ## Pull Request Overview This PR deprecates the `www` workspace in favor of `v4`, updating all references throughout the contributing documentation to reflect the new structure and package naming conventions. - Renamed workspace from `www` to `v4` across all documentation - Updated package references from `shadcn-ui` to `shadcn` - Simplified CLI development workflow by removing unnecessary steps --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Pull Request Overview

This PR refactors the registry system to support multiple styles, moving from a single hardcoded "new-york-v4" style to a multi-style architecture. The refactoring introduces a styles configuration system, updates the registry index structure to be multi-dimensional (style → component), and modifies the build process to generate separate registry files per style.

Key changes:
- Introduced `STYLES` array and related functions in `registry/styles.ts` to manage available styles
- Restructured the registry index from `Record<string, any>` to `Record<string, Record<string, any>>` for multi-style support
- Updated all component preview and viewer functions to accept a `style` parameter

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 24 out of 35 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts | Refactored build script to process multiple styles, validate schemas, and generate per-style registry files |
| apps/v4/registry/styles.ts | New file defining available styles and utility functions |
| apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts | Updated imports to use new structure with relative paths |
| apps/v4/registry/themes.ts | Updated import path for base colors |
| apps/v4/registry/__index__.tsx | Restructured to nest component definitions under style keys |
| apps/v4/lib/registry.ts | Added `style` parameter to registry functions and introduced `getRegistryItems` helper |
| apps/v4/lib/rehype.ts | Updated to use active style when accessing registry components |
| apps/v4/lib/llm.ts | Added `style` parameter to MDX processing |
| apps/v4/lib/colors.ts | Updated import path |
| apps/v4/lib/themes.ts | Updated import path |
| apps/v4/lib/blocks.ts | Refactored to collect blocks across all styles |
| apps/v4/components/theme-customizer.tsx | Updated import path for base colors |
| apps/v4/component

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR upgrades the project to Next.js 16.0.0, React 19.2.0, and related dependencies, along with updating ESLint configuration to use the new flat config format and making various code improvements for type safety and React best practices.

- Upgraded Next.js from 15.3.1 to 16.0.0, React from 19.1.0 to 19.2.0, and fumadocs packages to latest versions
- Migrated ESLint configuration from FlatCompat to native flat config with `eslint/config`
- Improved React hooks usage by fixing dependency arrays and replacing deprecated patterns
- Replaced type assertions and TODO comments with proper type handling

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 24 out of 26 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| package.json | Added pnpm overrides for @types/react and @types/react-dom versions |
| apps/v4/package.json | Upgraded Next.js, React, fumadocs packages, zod, and removed @eslint/eslintrc dependency |
| apps/v4/tsconfig.json | Changed jsx mode from "preserve" to "react-jsx" and added dev types to includes |
| apps/v4/eslint.config.mjs | Migrated to native ESLint flat config format with updated rule configuration |
| apps/v4/next.config.mjs | Added experimental turbopack file system cache configuration |
| apps/v4/source.config.ts | Commented out schema validation code pending zod v4 upgrade |
| apps/v4/hooks/use-mobile.ts | Fixed missing dependency in useEffect hook |
| apps/v4/hooks/use-layout.tsx | Wrapped computed value in useMemo to prevent recalculation |
| apps/v4/hooks/use-is-mac.ts | Refactored to use lazy initialization with SSR check instead of useEffect |
| apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/typography-table.tsx | Fixed apostrophe escaping in JSX strings |
| apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/sheet-side.tsx | Fixed apostrophe escaping in JSX strings |
| apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/form-rhf-pa

title: docs(calendar): add timezone fix for date selection offset issue
body: Added a new section to the Calendar documentation explaining how to fix the issue where selecting a date highlights the previous day. The section includes an example showing how to detect the user’s local timezone and pass it as a timeZone prop to the Calendar component to resolve the date offset problem.
author: preetsuthar17

commits.message:
docs(calendar): add timezone fix for date selection offset issue

Added a new section to the Calendar documentation explaining how to fix the issue where selecting a date highlights the previous day. The section includes an example showing how to detect the user’s local timezone and pass it as a timeZone prop to the Calendar component to resolve the date offset problem.

### Pull Request: Fix TypeScript import issues and update sidebar config

#### Summary

This PR addresses two issues:

1. **Fix incorrect imports of `VariantProps` from `class-variance-authority`**  
   - Updated all relevant components to use `import type` for `VariantProps`.
   - Resolves a TypeScript error when `"verbatimModuleSyntax": true` is enabled (which Vite sets by default).

2.  **Update `.json` sidebar configuration files under `apps/www/public/r/styles/**`**  
   - Ensures the docs site sidebar reflects the updated component structure.

#### Why?

- Prevents type import errors during Vite + TypeScript builds.
- Keeps documentation and navigation aligned with actual source code.

#### Validation

- Ran `pnpm lint`, `pnpm build`, successfully.

title: docs: fix next/link import syntax in multiple components
body:
Fixes #8425

## Problem

Multiple components show incorrect Link import:
```tsx
import { Link } from "next/link" // ❌ Wrong
```

This causes TypeScript errors when users copy the examples.

## Solution

Fixed to use default import:
```tsx
import Link from "next/link" // ✅ Correct
```

## Files Fixed (5)

- Button
- Badge  
- Breadcrumb (v4 & www)
- Navigation Menu

## Note

While other PRs fix Button only, this addresses **all affected components** in one go.
author: serhatx1

commits.message:
docs: fix next/link import syntax in component examples

Fix incorrect named import syntax for Next.js Link component.

Changed from:
  import { Link } from "next/link"

To correct default import:
  import Link from "next/link"

Next.js Link is a default export, not a named export. The incorrect
syntax causes TypeScript error:
'Module "next/link" has no exported member "Link"'

Affected files:
- Button component docs
- Badge component docs
- Breadcrumb component docs (v4 and www)
- Navigation Menu component docs

Fixes issue where users copying code snippets get immediate errors.

This PR changes the import of ToasterProps in apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/sonner.tsx

The error I was getting 

sonner.tsx:2 Uncaught SyntaxError: The requested module '/node_modules/.vite/deps/sonner.js?v=8cb40f71' does not provide an export named 'ToasterProps' (at sonner.tsx:2:29)

Fixed it by changing
import { Toaster as Sonner, ToasterProps } from "sonner";
To
import { Toaster as Sonner, type ToasterProps } from "sonner";

title: fix(field): return null when errors are empty
body:
## Fix: FieldErrors returns `null` for empty or `[undefined]` arrays

This PR updates the `FieldErrors` component to improve its handling of error arrays received from React Hook Form and similar libraries.


- Fixes #8369 

### Why

- `error` props can have `[undefined]` or `[]` or `undefined` values according to its type definition.
  - scenario: when used with React hook form, the errors are passed as `<FieldError errors={[fieldState.error]} />` thus passing `[undefined]` when there are no errors.
- Previously, the component would still render, causing unnecessary UI elements.
- Returning `null` ensures the component does not render when there are no actual errors, keeping the UI clean and consistent.

### What Changed

- If the `errors` prop is an empty array (`[]`) or an array containing only `undefined` (e.g., `[undefined]`), the component now returns `null` and does not render any output.

### Impact

- Prevents unwanted rendering of error components when there are no errors.
- Improves compatibility with React Hook Form and similar form libraries.
- No need to explicitly add extra conditional check to have this functionality
author: krishnagkmit

commits.message: fix(field): return null when errors are empty

title: fix(input-group-textarea): prevent child elements from overflowing by…
body:
### Problem
The Textarea container element was missing a min-w-0 style.
As a result, when typing long text, the layout could overflow its parent container.

### Solution
Add min-w-0 to the Textarea container to ensure it respects its parent width and prevents overflow.

### Changes
- Added min-w-0 to the Textarea container class.
- Ensured long text input no longer causes layout overflow.

### Modified: 
- apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/input-group.tsx
- apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/blocks/new-components-01/components/ui/input-group.tsx


### Before
```tsx
function InputGroup({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="input-group"
      role="group"
      className={cn(
        "group/input-group border-input dark:bg-input/30 relative flex w-full items-center rounded-md border shadow-xs transition-[color,box-shadow] outline-none",
        "h-9 has-[>textarea]:h-auto",
//...
```

### After
```tsx
function InputGroup({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="input-group"
      role="group"
      className={cn(
        "group/input-group border-input dark:bg-input/30 relative flex w-full items-center rounded-md border shadow-xs transition-[color,box-shadow] outline-none",
        "h-9 min-w-0 has-[>textarea]:h-auto",
//...
```

Related Issue
#8335 

Screenshots (from test page)

### Before (Error)
![before](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ebc9c51d-e479-48c5-a2ea-641c5fced9cd)

### After
![after](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5e0e068b-0670-44d7-bbc1-500f8d77ae19)
author: HyeongSeoku

commits.message: fix(input-group-textarea): prevent child elements from overflowing by adding min-w-0

title: feat(shadcn): fix transformCssVars function
body:
This fixes a bug in the transformCssVars transformer (when `tailwind.cssVariables: false`) that was rewriting all string literals in a file.
As a result, some strings ended up with nested quotes, for example:

```ts
import React from "'react'"
const icon = "'suffix'"
```

instead of staying untouched.

See #8185.

**Cause**

`transformCssVars` was rewriting all string literals using raw text APIs:
- it read strings with `getText()` (which includes the surrounding quotes),
- then wrote them back with `replaceWithText(...)` (adding quotes again).

When the mapping was a no-op (e.g., for non-class strings like import specifiers), this produced nested quotes.

**Fix**

Use literal-safe APIs:
- read with `getLiteralText()` (value without quotes),
- write with `setLiteralValue(...)` (ts-morph handles quoting correctly).

No scope changes to the transformer, only safer literal handling.
author: imskyleen

reviews.body: @imskyleen This looks good. Yes looks like you caught and fixed another bug with prefix and cssVariables: false. Thank you.

title: feat(charts): support legend and tooltip type none
body:
Recharts supports `legendType="none"` and `tooltipType="none"` on different chart types. The following changes allow us to define which keys are supported in `ChartTooltipContent` _and_ `ChartLegendContent`.

Simply adding `payload.filter((item) => item.type !== "none")` before mapping over it.

The **[legendType](https://recharts.org/en-US/api/Area#legendType)** is well documented whereas the **tooltipType** isn't (while still valid recharts property).

```jsx
<Area
  dataKey="error"
  stroke="var(--color-error)"
  // NOTE: hides the dataKey "error" from the `ChartTooltipContent` and `ChartLegendContent`
  legendType="none"
  tooltipType="none"
/>
```
author: mxkaske

commits.message: feat(charts): support legend and tooltip type none

title: feat: add @elements to trusted registries
body:
### Description
This PR adds @elements to the list of trusted registries.

Elements is an open-source registry of full-stack components for modern web apps. It goes beyond UI by providing complete integrations (auth, payments, AI, jobs, files, database) built with React, TypeScript, and TailwindCSS. Each component is designed to be installed in seconds and tweaked easily, fitting naturally into the shadcn/ui ecosystem.

### Motivation

Developers using shadcn/ui often need more than static UI, they need production-ready flows and integrations. By including Elements in the trusted registries, users get access to full-stack, end-to-end components that speed up shipping without sacrificing design or developer experience.

It’s the next logical step of the @shadcn/ui ecosystem.

### Links
website: https://www.tryelements.dev/
github: https://github.com/crafter-station/elements
organization: https://crafterstation.com
author: https://railly.dev
author: Railly

title: feat: add @smoothui to trusted registries
body:
## Description
This PR adds **@smoothui** to the list of trusted registries.

**SmoothUI** is a library of ready-to-use UI components built with **React, TailwindCSS, and Framer Motion**, focused on providing microinteractions and polished UI experiences. It’s designed to be easily copied, pasted, and integrated into projects, fitting naturally into the shadcn/ui ecosystem.

## Changes
- Added `@smoothui` to the trusted registries list.

## Motivation
Developers using shadcn/ui often look for high-quality component libraries that complement the design system. By including SmoothUI in the trusted registries, users will have access to animated, interactive components that can enhance their applications while maintaining consistency with shadcn/ui.

## References
- [SmoothUI website](https://smoothui.dev)

title: fix(cli): detect vinxi-based frameworks (@tanstack/start, SolidStart, ...)
body:
This PR adds CLI support for [vinxi](https://github.com/nksaraf/vinxi)-based projects, such as [@tanstack/start](https://tanstack.com/start/latest) and [Solid Start](https://start.solidjs.com/).

Thanks to the fact that those projects are essentially Vite projects, not many changes were necessary, and adding the `app.config.*` to the detection logic is all that was really necessary. Just to be on the safe side, I also added a simple check that the said file does contain a `defineConfig` string (and is actually a Vinxi-based config), as the name `app.config.*` tends to be quite generic.

This has been tested in a barebones @tanstack/start project, and the result [can be viewed here](https://github.com/KitsuneDev/shadcn-cli-tanstack-example). Each commit represents a step of the setup process, following the instructions [from the docs.](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/installation/vite)
author: KitsuneDev

title: fix(components): resolve duplicate id conflict in calendar-24 component
body:
## Description

Fixes duplicate HTML ID conflict in calendar-24 component where both date and time picker elements could potentially share the same ID.

## Changes

- Changed Button `id` from 'date' to 'date-picker'
- Changed Input `id` from 'time' to 'time-picker'
- Updated corresponding Label `htmlFor` attributes to match the new unique IDs

## Impact

- Resolves potential HTML validation errors caused by duplicate IDs
- Fixes accessibility issues with form labels
- Maintains all existing functionality while ensuring proper label-to-input associations
- Uses more descriptive and unique ID names

## Testing

- ✅ Manual verification of label clicking behavior
- ✅ Accessibility testing confirms proper label associations
- ✅ Component functionality remains unchanged

Fixes #7561
author: xabierlameiro

reviews.body: @xabierlameiro Thank you. Can you run `pnpm registry:build` from the root, commit and push please?

commits.message:
fix(components): resolve duplicate id conflict in calendar-24 component

- Changed Button id from 'date' to 'date-picker'
- Changed Input id from 'time' to 'time-picker'
- Updated corresponding Label htmlFor attributes to match new unique IDs

Fixes #7561
chore: rebuild registry after calendar-24 fixes

title: feat(shadcn): add support for updating dependencies with expo-cli for RN compatibility
body:
## Description

The aim of this PR is to enable using the registry to update dependencies in Expo (React Native) projects. This update extends compatibility to React Native workflows using Expo.

### Why 

React Native projects need exact package versions to work. Expo CLI helps install the right ones automatically, so using it avoids version issues. See the [expo docs](https://docs.expo.dev/more/expo-cli/#install) for more details.


### Changes

- feat(shadcn): Add expo as a supported framework.

- feat(shadcn): Add support for dependency updates via expo-cli to ensure correct versions for React Native.

- refactor(shadcn): Extract and modularize dependency installation logic.

- fix: Update contributing command to use the correct registry.
author: mrzachnugent

commits.message: feat(shadcn): add support for updating dependencies with expo-cli for RN compatibility
feat(shadcn): add expo as a framework
fix: update the contributing command for registry

title: chore(vscode): configure Tailwind IntelliSense for monorepo UI
body:
Fixes an issue where Tailwind CSS IntelliSense wouldn't work in app folders (`/apps/web`, etc.) when using the default `shadcn/turborepo` setup.

By default, the VS Code Tailwind extension only detects the config inside `packages/ui`, causing class suggestions to fail in other packages.
This PR adds a `.vscode/settings.json` file that explicitly points to the correct config file:

```json
{
  "tailwindCSS.experimental.configFile": "packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css"
}
```

### Context

I raised the issue here on Twitter:
🔗 [https://x.com/steellgold/status/1934295365760512388](https://x.com/steellgold/status/1934295365760512388)

It was solved thanks to this suggestion:
💡 [https://x.com/Mahmut\_Jomaa/status/1934316610719830421](https://x.com/Mahmut_Jomaa/status/1934316610719830421)
author: Steellgold

commits.message: chore(vscode): set custom Tailwind config path for monorepo UI

title: refactor(sidebar): improve setOpen callback logic
body:
- Simplify state handling
- Ensure consistent cookie setting
- Added ability for users to persist sidebar state via cookies
- Update sidebar.mdx doc with SSR persistence instructions
- Run pnpm registry:build to update component
author: lumpinif

reviews.body: Excellent fix @lumpinif. I'll merge (I made some small doc updates)

commits.message:
refactor(sidebar): improve setOpen callback logic
- Simplify state handling
- Ensure consistent cookie setting
- Added ability for users to persist sidebar state via cookies
- Update sidebar.mdx doc with SSR persistence instructions
- Run pnpm registry:build to update component
